text.title: Anti-patterns to avoid
text.summary: Failure modes observed across faion-network batches; avoid each by following the cited rule.

antipattern.description: Long inline prompt text in the orchestrator's `Agent` spawn message instead of a path to a `prompts/*.md` file.
antipattern.reason: Behaviour drifts run-to-run, prompts cannot be reviewed or diffed, and parallel spawns desynchronize.

antipattern.description: One subagent doing two phases at once (e.g., executing AND capturing AND delivering).
antipattern.reason: Concerns blur, errors in one phase mask another, and re-runs become impossible. Keep one phase per prompt file.

antipattern.description: Skipping the clarification batch and asking the user a fresh question every time something is uncertain.
antipattern.reason: The user's attention is finite. Fragmenting questions degrades answer quality and erodes trust in the orchestrator's planning.

antipattern.description: Ignoring the quota gate after a few successful dispatches because "we're moving fast."
antipattern.reason: A long batch without quota gating starves later batches in the same 5-hour window. The cost is paid by the next session, not this one.

antipattern.description: Running multiple parallel executors against the main working tree instead of isolated worktrees.
antipattern.reason: Concurrent edits race on uncommitted local state (`scheduled_tasks.lock`, `.aidocs/catalog/improve-*.txt`) and on `.git/index`. Worktrees are cheap; use them.

antipattern.description: Auto-resolving a merge conflict with `--ours` or `--theirs`.
antipattern.reason: Silently destroys one side's intent. Use the resolver subagent which reads both sides' SDD specs and combines them.

antipattern.description: Bypassing a failing pre-commit hook with `--no-verify` (especially the `CHANGELOG.md` check).
antipattern.reason: The hook exists because the project decided that check is mandatory. Fix the underlying issue (add the changelog entry, fix the lint error) and re-commit.

antipattern.description: Amending a commit after a hook failure instead of creating a new one.
antipattern.reason: If the hook failed, the commit did not happen — `--amend` then mutates the previous commit, often destroying earlier work. Always create a new commit.

antipattern.description: Merging feature branches into `main` without `flock` serialization.
antipattern.reason: Two concurrent fast-forward merges race on `.git/HEAD`; one silently loses. The merge step is the only single-threaded part of the wave — wrap it.
Telegram downscales the image; text becomes unreadable and the bug area is a needle in a haystack.
They convey nothing the file name does not already convey. Captions must explain the bug or the fix in plain Ukrainian.

antipattern.description: Pretending a defensive fix has a visible diff by inventing fixture data without disclosing it.
antipattern.reason: The reviewer is being shown evidence under false pretences. State explicitly when a fix is defensive and any data was simulated.

antipattern.description: Pushing or deploying because the user authorized it once in a previous run.
antipattern.reason: Authorization is per-run, per-target. A blanket "yes" yesterday is not consent today. Re-confirm via `AskUserQuestion`.

antipattern.description: Committing local-only state (`scheduled_tasks.lock`, `.aidocs/catalog/improve-queue.txt`, `/tmp/...`) by using `git add -A`.
antipattern.reason: Stage explicit paths. Bulk staging is the leading cause of secret leaks and noisy diffs in this repo.
